Proposed KPIs: Burn Multiple (Net burn / Net new ARR)
Rationale: Links growth directly to capital efficiency, enabling cross-portfolio comparability regardless of scale.
Better captures quality of growth by incorporating churn, contraction, and expansion rather than absolute revenue growth.
Smooths short-term volatility and provides a clearer signal of underlying growth momentum than single-quarter growth.
Combines growth and profitability into a single efficiency metric more relevant at Simetrik’s scale than raw MRR.
Directly measures sales efficiency and payback, which is more actionable than runway alone for a scaling SaaS.
column5: How much ARR is generated for every dollar spent on S&M

Rationale: Better reflects economic scale and monetization than policy count, which ignores ticket size.
Captures customer lifetime value and product stickiness, which new policy volume does not.
More relevant for a roll-up model as it captures working capital intensity and liquidity risk, not just scale.
Shifts focus from survival to sustainable growth efficiency as the company scales.
Better reflects balance between growth and operating leverage than client count alone.
Better monetization signal than user count, which does not correlate with revenue.
Directly measures product usage and value delivered, which drives retention and upsell potential.
Already showing NDR in standardized metrics, churn helps remove pixelation from upsells
Better reflects monetization depth and usage intensity than aggregate MRR.

Proposed KPIs: API Calls or Total Payment Volume
Rationale: Captures real platform usage and engagement, not just commercial contracts.
Shifts focus from activity volume to economic value generated per customer.
Improves visibility on revenue durability and predictability versus project-based income.
Already ask for Runway. Yield on portfolio better reflects asset quality and cash generation than portfolio size alone.
Focuses on executed volume rather than funnel top-of-funnel noise.
Separates legacy or third party portfolio servicing revenue from new underwriting quality, improving revenue visibility.
Highlights expansion dynamics and customer value evolution beyond headline MRR.
Already ask for NDR. New logo arr separates new growth from expansion, improving go-to-market signal clarity.
Better captures portfolio risk and capital preservation than client count.
Shows the progress better toward a more scalable, higher-multiple SaaS-led business model.(new product Terminal)
Better reflects pricing power and customer sophistication than aggregate MRR.
